w:r.w:t: For Claude or any AI agent picking up this project mid-build

Sajiir POS is a multi-tenant, offline-capable Point of Sale system built specifically for Kenyan retail businesses — supermarkets, general trade, and FMCG merchants. It is built with Django and is deployed as a single codebase that serves multiple client businesses, each isolated in their own PostgreSQL schema.
A cashier can scan items, accept payment (Cash, M-Pesa STK Push, or Card), and print a KRA-compliant receipt in under 30 seconds
A manager can write off expired or spoiled stock with a full audit trail
A business owner can see consolidated revenue across all branches in one dashboard
Sales continue even when the internet goes down (offline-first via Service Worker + IndexedDB)
Every sale is submitted to KRA eTIMS for VAT compliance
Kenyan supermarkets and retail shops (single or multi-branch)
VAT-registered businesses required to use KRA eTIMS
Cashiers using desktop browsers (not mobile, not a native app)
Stock is a ledger, not a counter — every movement has a reason, a who, and a when
Carton-to-unit conversion built in — buy 10 cartons of 24 units, stock shows 240 units
M-Pesa STK Push is native, not bolted on
Offline mode is a first-class feature, not an afterthought
KRA eTIMS compliance is baked in from day one
Per-branch stock — a product's stock at Branch A is independent of Branch B
API layer for all browser→server calls
WebSockets — M-Pesa callback to cashier UI
Background tasks: eTIMS, alerts, expiry checks
Offline mode: IndexedDB queue, sync on reconnect
Originally requested — NOT used. Tailwind used instead.

The UI uses Tailwind CSS, not Bootstrap 5 as originally requested. The agent substituted it early and built all templates consistently with Tailwind. Switching now would require rewriting every template. Recommendation: keep Tailwind.
The repository root contains both the Django project and some leftover Google AI Studio scaffold files that must be deleted.
│ ├── frontend_urls.py ← Cashier template view URLs [NEEDS ADDING]
3.2 Files to DELETE (Google AI Studio leftovers)

These files have nothing to do with Django and must be removed from the repo: src/ (entire folder), index.html (root), package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json. They are React/Vite/TypeScript files from the Google AI Studio template that was used to start the project.
The following table shows the current state of each component as of the last session.
With eTIMS branch code and device serial
With KRA tax_type, carton conversion fields
Full atomic transaction, idempotency, loyalty pts
Creates StockMovements, batches, updates PO status
Assigns fake invoice number — real API not wired
Stub — returns fake response. Real Daraja not wired
Create sale, handles M-Pesa pending flow via cache
by_barcode action exists but wrong URL pattern
Basic CRUD, no open/close business logic
Router registered but sync endpoint not added
Code exists in api/v1/sync.py, not wired in urls
Written in last session — NOT yet in repo
Written in last session — NOT yet in repo
New version written — NOT yet replaced in repo
New version written — NOT yet replaced in repo
Barcode scan, cart, payments, offline save
accounts and tenants apps have initial migrations
Creates public tenant, main tenant, superuser
4 admin files reference non-existent fields — broken
No login page exists — cashiers cannot authenticate
These are the exact problems experienced in the last session, in priority order.
The root URL "/" has no route. pos_project/config/urls.py only has /admin/ and /api/v1/. No frontend URL pattern exists, so visiting the site shows Django's 404 debug page.
Fix: Add frontend_urls.py to the config folder and include it in urls.py.
from django.contrib.auth import views as auth_views

Four admin.py files reference model fields that do not exist, causing Django to silently fail registering those ModelAdmins. Affected files: apps/branches/admin.py (references Branch.code, Branch.location), apps/products/admin.py (references Product.unit_price, ProductVariant), apps/sales/admin.py (references Sale.receipt_number, Sale.payment_method, SaleItem.subtotal), apps/inventory/admin.py (references movement_type, created_by, reorder_level).
Fix — replace each broken admin.py with the correct field names:
# Remove ProductVariant — it does not exist on the model
After fixing, run: python manage.py check — it will report any remaining field errors before you open the browser.
BLOCKER 3: Cashier has no way to log in
There is no login template or login URL. @login_required on the checkout view redirects to /login/ but that URL does not exist, causing another 404.
Also create frontend/templates/base_minimal.html — a version of base.html without the session timer and nav links, used for pre-login pages (login, 404, 500).
BLOCKER 4: No tenant for localhost — django-tenants 404

django-tenants routes requests based on the subdomain/domain. If no Tenant or Domain record exists for "localhost", every request returns 404 regardless of the URL. setup_pos.py must be run once to seed the public tenant and domain.
Fix — run this once on a fresh database:
setup_pos.py creates: the public tenant (schema_name="public", domain="localhost"), the main store tenant (schema_name="main", domain="main.localhost"), and the superuser admin@pos.com / admin123.
After running setup_pos.py, check the database: SELECT schema_name FROM tenants_tenant; and SELECT domain FROM tenants_domain; — you should see "public" and "localhost".
BLOCKER 5: views_frontend.py not in the repo

The cashier view functions (checkout, session_open, session_close, product_lookup) were written in the last Claude session but were not saved to the repository. They exist only in the conversation history.
Fix: Create apps/sales/views_frontend.py with the following functions:
session_open(request) — renders session_open.html, creates CashSession on POST
checkout(request) — renders checkout.html, redirects to session_open if no open session
session_close(request) — renders session_close.html with Z-report preview, closes session on POST
z_report(request, session_id) — printable Z-report for a closed session
product_lookup(request) — AJAX endpoint: GET ?barcode=xxx → JSON with product + stock
The full implementation is in the previous conversation turn. Copy it directly.
The project is successful when the following things all work without errors:
python manage.py runserver starts without errors
Logging in as admin@pos.com shows the session open page
http://localhost:8000/admin/ shows all models — not just Groups
Cashier opens a session with an opening float
Cashier sees the checkout screen with barcode input auto-focused
Scanning a barcode (or typing SKU + Enter) adds the product to cart
Cart updates in real time — item name, quantity, line total, VAT, grand total
Cash payment: entering tendered amount shows correct change, Complete Sale saves
M-Pesa payment: entering phone sends STK Push, confirming unlocks Complete Sale
Sale is saved to the database with correct StockMovement records
Offline: going offline queues the sale in IndexedDB; reconnecting syncs it
Cashier closes session, sees session totals, Z-report prints
Every sale has a KRA eTIMS invoice number and QR code on the receipt
Products can be received via GRN with carton-to-unit conversion
Expiring stock is flagged 7 days before expiry date
Stock write-offs (spoilage, expiry, theft) create AuditLog records
Manager can see branch dashboard with revenue, tax, and payment breakdown
All tests pass: pytest runs green
localhost:8000 returns 404 — no frontend URL routing
Django admin shows only "Groups" — broken admin.py field references
Visiting /login/ crashes — no login template exists
Stock goes negative — means select_for_update is not working or InsufficientStockError is swallowed
The same offline sale creates two Sale records — offline_uuid uniqueness check is broken
BranchStock diverges from StockMovement sum — means someone wrote to BranchStock directly instead of going through StockService
These rules exist because violating them causes data corruption, not just bugs. They are hard constraints, not guidelines.
Never hard-delete products, sales, customers, or suppliers. Use is_active=False. Historical sales must always resolve their original product name and price.

Stock is a ledger. BranchStock.quantity is maintained only by the post_save signal on StockMovement. Never write to BranchStock directly from a view, serializer, or service. Always call StockService.adjust().
All sale completion logic lives in SaleService.complete() inside transaction.atomic(). Views call this method — they never create Sale, SaleItem, Payment, or StockMovement directly.
Offline sync is idempotent. Check offline_uuid against the Sale table before calling SaleService.complete(). Duplicates must return already_synced silently, never raise an error.
The M-Pesa callback view has no authentication. It must always return HTTP 200 with {"ResultCode": 0}. Non-200 causes Daraja to retry for hours.
GRNItem carton conversion happens in GRNItem.save() — not in views or services. quantity_sale_units = quantity_purchase_units * product.units_per_purchase.
Celery tasks that query the database must call schema_context(schema_name) first. Without it, the ORM hits the public schema and finds nothing.
AuditLog is append-only. Never update or delete AuditLog rows. Write via AuditMixin or AuditService.log() — never from views directly.
Do these in order. Do not skip ahead. Each step unblocks the next.
Fix apps/branches/admin.py — change list_display to use actual field names
Fix apps/products/admin.py — remove ProductVariant, use selling_price not unit_price
Fix apps/sales/admin.py — use sale_number not receipt_number, remove payment_method
Fix apps/inventory/admin.py — use reason not movement_type, performed_by not created_by
Run: python manage.py check — must show 0 errors before proceeding
Create apps/sales/views_frontend.py with 5 view functions (from conversation history)
Add LOGIN_URL, LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL, LOGOUT_REDIRECT_URL to settings/base.py
Verify: python manage.py runserver, visit http://localhost:8000/ — should redirect to /login/
Verify: log in at http://localhost:8000/login/ with admin@pos.com / admin123
Verify: /admin/ now shows Branches, Products, Sales, etc. not just Groups
Step 4 — Add opening stock so the till has products (20 min)
Create a Product with a barcode (e.g. 5000112637922 = Coca-Cola 500ml)
Create a Branch (e.g. Main Branch, etims_branch_code = NBR)
Create a TenantUser linking admin@pos.com to the Main Store tenant with role = cashier
Create opening stock via StockService.adjust() in Django shell or a management command
Step 5 — Test the full till flow (30 min)
Scan the product barcode on the till screen
Verify: Sale appears in Django admin with correct stock deduction
These relationships must be understood before touching any business logic.
Product has no stock field — stock lives in BranchStock (per branch)
Every stock change creates a StockMovement row (append-only)
The post_save signal on StockMovement updates BranchStock using assignment (=), not +=
StockService.adjust() is the ONLY permitted way to create a StockMovement
A Sale belongs to a CashSession (cashier's shift)
A Sale has many SaleItems (price snapshot — never changes after creation)
A Sale has many Payments (Cash + M-Pesa + Card on the same sale is valid)
Creating a Sale triggers StockMovement(reason="sale") for each SaleItem
After the transaction commits, sign_sale_etims.delay() is queued
PurchaseOrder raised to a Supplier for a Branch
GoodsReceivedNote recorded when goods arrive (may or may not reference a PO)
GRNService.receive() creates StockMovement(reason="purchase") + ProductBatch if expiry tracked
Everything else → per-tenant schema (Branch, Product, Sale, Stock, etc.)
A user can belong to multiple tenants with different roles in each
TenantUser.branch restricts a cashier/manager to a specific branch (null = all branches)
All variables must be in a .env file in the project root. Never commit .env.
Django secret key — generate with: python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
True for dev, False for production
Lipa Na M-Pesa passkey from Daraja
Public HTTPS URL — ngrok in dev, real domain in prod
# Copy env file and fill in values
docker-compose exec web python manage.py migrate_schemas --shared
docker-compose exec web python manage.py migrate_schemas
cp .env.example .env # fill in DATABASE_URL and REDIS_URL
# In another terminal — start Celery worker

Expected: test_stock_adjust_creates_movement_and_updates_branch_stock → PASS, test_stock_adjust_insufficient_stock → PASS, test_sale_service_complete → PASS (if Celery task is mocked), test_sale_service_idempotence → PASS
Important decisions already locked in — do not revisit these without a strong reason.
UI framework: Tailwind CSS (not Bootstrap 5 as originally requested). All templates use Tailwind consistently. Switching would require rewriting every template for no functional gain.
Database: PostgreSQL with django-tenants schema-per-tenant isolation. SQLite is not supported.
Auth: Email-based (not username). JWT for the API, Django session auth for template views.
Offline mode: Service Worker + IndexedDB. Every sale gets a browser-generated UUID to prevent duplicates on sync.
Payments: Cash confirmed immediately. M-Pesa waits for Daraja callback before marking confirmed. Card is manual reference entry.
Stock ledger: BranchStock is a summary, StockMovement is the truth. This pattern must not change.
KRA eTIMS: Every sale must be signed. The ETIMSClient is a stub — real KRA API credentials needed for production.
Carton conversion: Happens in GRNItem.save(). The view/form collects purchase-unit quantities; the model computes sale-unit quantities automatically.
Return approval threshold: KES 5,000 — returns above this require manager approval (field approved_by on Return model).
Loyalty points: 1 point per KES 100 spent, rounded down. No expiry for now.
Design decisions that must NOT be changed

BranchStock is never written to directly. Only the StockMovement signal updates it. If you find code that does BranchStock.objects.filter(...).update(quantity=...) or branch_stock.quantity = X; branch_stock.save() outside the signal — that is a bug.
SaleService.complete() is the single point of entry for creating a sale. If you find a view that creates a Sale object directly, that is a bug.
The M-Pesa callback view has no authentication classes. This is intentional. Safaricom cannot authenticate. Protect it with HMAC verification instead (optional, see MPESA_CALLBACK_SECRET env var).
— End of Sajiir POS Project Guide —
Read Section 5 (Blockers) and Section 8 (Next Steps) first
